content.full_text:
July 21, 1976

Honorable Morton L. Barker, (Ret.)
2381-2H Via Mariposa West
Laguna Hills, California 92653

Dear Judge Barker:

This letter is in response to your recent inquiry concerning the financial disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act for retired judges receiving assignments from the court.

We are currently drafting a definition of "judges of courts of record" for the purpose of determining who have filing obligations under the law. The Commission will review a staff recommendation at its August meeting and I will let you know the decision of the Commission as soon as it is available.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

July 6, 1976


Technical Assistance Section
Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

I am a retired judge. I served on assignment for
several years after retirement.

Is it necessary for me to file a financial disclosure
report? Will it be necessary to do so if I should receive
future assignments?

Thank you.

Yours very truly,

[signature]
Morton L. Barker
9238-2 H Via Mariposa
Laguna Hills, Cal
92653

sections.question: Is it necessary for me to file a financial disclosure report? Will it be necessary to do so if I should receive future assignments?
sections.conclusion: We are currently drafting a definition of "judges of courts of record" for the purpose of determining who have filing obligations under the law. The Commission will review a staff recommendation at its August meeting and I will let you know the decision of the Commission as soon as it is available.
sections.question_synthetic: A retired judge who has served on court assignments is inquiring whether they have financial disclosure filing obligations under the Political Reform Act, both for past assignments and potential future assignments.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is still in the process of drafting definitions to determine filing obligations for judges and will provide a definitive answer after the Commission's August meeting.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality is poor with OCR errors in the scanned image. The incoming request from Judge Barker (dated July 6, 1976) and FPPC response (dated July 21, 1976) are both presented. The response indicates the matter is pending further Commission action, making this a partial/interim response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is it necessary for me to file a financial disclosure report? Will it be necessary to do so if I should receive future assignments?

CONCLUSION: We are currently drafting a definition of "judges of courts of record" for the purpose of determining who have filing obligations under the law. The Commission will review a staff recommendation at its August meeting and I will let you know the decision of the Commission as soon as it is available.

embedding.summary: A retired judge inquired about financial disclosure filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC responded that it is currently drafting definitions and will provide guidance after its August meeting.
